content: Just as linguistic reflection (the human cognitive/educational meaning of reflexion) relies on stepping back from immediate action to re-examine one’s own output from external perspectives, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by explicitly grounding their reflection routines in the same structured reflective writing exercises used in high school literacy education. This cross-pollination creates a new path to make abstract LLM self-correction more systematic and aligned with how humans learn to refine their own thinking.
